headline: Hungary HOLD at 37.5 / Developing (May 9 proposal pinned) — day 2 post-inauguration evidence is confirmatory of the May 9 +9.4 trajectory, not additive. Fidesz condemned Magyar's Sulyok-resignation demand as 'unworthy of a prime minister' (expected obstructionist opposition posture given Tisza's two-thirds supermajority); Sulyok has not resigned (compliance deadline May 31). Cabinet formation on track for mid-May. EU funds plan to be submitted May 27 after Magyar–von der Leyen talks; EU officials describe €10.4B target as 'ambitious but possible' but Commission not ready to release all frozen funds immediately. No new enacted action above the day-1 baseline that drove the +9.4 May 9 delta. Per prompt: 'pin at 37.5 unless new enacted action emerges. Day-2 statements alone are not sufficient for upward movement.' Confirmation. The Sulyok compliance deadline (May 31) remains the next material scoring trigger; June 9 30-day re-assessment unchanged.
key_evidence: May 10 2026 (in-window): Magyar demanded President Sulyok resign by May 31 in inaugural parliamentary address; Sulyok has not resigned. Fidesz parliamentary group leader Gulyas condemned the demand as 'raising serious doubts' and 'unworthy of a prime minister.' Sulyok dismissal status: SIGNALED + DEADLINE ACTIVE, not yet enacted.

May 10 2026 (in-window): Fidesz transitioned into opposition role with statement condemning Magyar's accountability demands — expected obstructionist opposition posture; Tisza retains two-thirds supermajority (141/199 seats).
May 10 2026 (in-window, Magyar statement): Cabinet formation targeted for mid-May (sworn in ~May 14-15); reforms could begin end of May.

May 11 2026 Reuters/US News (in-window edge): EU officials describe Hungary's race to access €10.4 billion as 'ambitious but possible'; plan to be submitted May 27 after Magyar–von der Leyen talks (May 25-26 summit). EU Commission not ready to release all frozen funds immediately — partial release expected.

Day-1 enacted actions credited May 9 (TV2 dismissal, EU flag, cabinet decentralization, Sulyok deadline announcement, €17B EU funds priority, 27 rule-of-law super-milestones framework) remain in effect. Day-2 evidence is confirmatory: no negative regression, no new enacted action above the day-1 baseline.

evidence.source: Pravda Hungary — Fidesz condemns Magyar's efforts to force Sulyok resignation
evidence.finding: Fidesz parliamentary group leader Gulyas condemned demand as 'unworthy of a prime minister'; Sulyok has not resigned; expected obstructionist opposition posture documented.

evidence.source: News Tribune — Magyar takes power vowing to return Hungary
evidence.finding: Day 2 confirmation of inaugural commitments; cabinet formation targeted mid-May; reforms could begin end of May.

evidence.source: US News — Hungary's race to access EU's 10.4 billion euros is 'ambitious but possible'
evidence.finding: EU officials assess €10.4B target as 'ambitious but possible'; plan submitted May 27 after Magyar–von der Leyen talks; partial fund release expected, not full immediate release.

evidence.source: Bloomberg — Hungary's new PM Magyar demands president quit in showdown
evidence.finding: Sulyok dismissal demand publicly framed as 'showdown' — Fidesz opposition response confirms day-2 obstructionist posture; supermajority capacity preserved.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained from May 9. No day-2 suffering-detection institutional changes. Anchor 2-3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 sustained from May 9. Day-2 confirmatory; no new E-dimension events; +0.5 from published 2.0 maintained on day-1 grounds.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 sustained from May 9. AC1 responsiveness: no operational response-time changes on day 2; AC4 resource mobilization: EU funds framework progressing (May 27 plan submission) but still signaled not enacted.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 sustained from May 9. No day-2 EQ-relevant changes; anti-LGBTQ statute remains in force; TV2 day-1 enacted action remains the only operationally tested EQ-dimension input.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained from May 9. B3 scope clarity: EU funds framework progressing (May 27 plan, Magyar-vdL talks May 25-26) sustains the day-1 enacted re-alignment posture; no negative regression.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 sustained from May 9. AB1 harm acknowledgment + AB2 correction willingness sustained on day-1 grounds; Sulyok deadline active but not enacted; Fidesz obstruction expected and does not reduce Magyar's mandate.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained from May 9. S2 long-term impact: 27 EU rule-of-law super-milestones with August 31 deadline = structural-change roadmap; cabinet formation timeline mid-May sustains operational momentum.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 sustained from May 9. I1 consistency under pressure: Magyar maintained Sulyok demand under Fidesz opposition pressure on day 2 — values-action posture sustained; INT remains the most pending dimension awaiting June 9 30-day evaluation.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 37.5 = May 9 proposal pinned. Material delta (+9.4 vs published; >5pt materiality threshold). Sub-threshold from band crossing (2.5 below Functional floor at 40.0). Developing band sustained. Not a boundary case (>1.0 from band boundary).

negativeSignals: Sulyok still in office (compliance deadline May 31); Fidesz obstructionist opposition active; EU Commission will not release all frozen funds immediately (partial release pattern); cabinet not yet formed at scan close; statute repeals not yet introduced; new government untested under sustained external pressure.
positiveSignals: Sulyok deadline framework remains active (Magyar did not retreat under Fidesz opposition pressure); cabinet formation timeline mid-May on track; EU funds plan submission scheduled May 27; Magyar–von der Leyen talks May 25-26 confirmed; EU officials' '€10.4B ambitious but possible' framing sustains the cooperative posture from day 1.
sector_context: Countries — EU Governance Transition. Day-2 evidence is confirmatory. Cross-reference: May 9 +9.4 upgrade proposal remains the material change; day-3 (May 11) and Sulyok deadline (May 31) are the next material scoring triggers. Hungary remains the highest-confidence positive band-change candidate in countries index this rotation pending June 9 30-day re-assessment.

EU funds plan submission timeline verified (May 27)

methodology_consistency_check: Day-2 assessment uses the same conservative-scoring rule applied May 9: day-1 enacted actions credited, day-2 confirmatory evidence does not produce additional dimensional movement absent new enacted action. Confirmed methodologically consistent with the prompt's explicit instruction: 'Day-2 statements alone are not sufficient for upward movement.'
